Title: Entra ID must limit privileges by requiring admin accounts are cloud-only
Description: Administrative accounts that are synced from on-premises Active Directory introduce risk because the on-premises environment could be compromised, leading to a cloud compromise. Admin accounts must be cloud-only to ensure the cloud environment is not dependent on potentially compromised on-premises infrastructure.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must limit emergency admin accounts to two
Description: Emergency accounts are administrator accounts established in response to crisis situations where the need for immediate administrative access is required. Conditions for emergency account activation are outlined in the organization contingency plan. Limiting the number of emergency accounts reduces the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must limit global admin role membership to 2-4 users
Description: The Global Administrator role has full access to all administrative features in Azure AD and services that use Azure AD identities. Limiting global admin role assignments to between 2 and 4 users reduces the risk of compromise while ensuring coverage for administrative operations.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must use Privileged Identity Management for role management
Description: Without using Privileged Identity Management (PIM), there is increased risk of a permanent privileged role assignment being compromised. PIM enables just-in-time role activation, requiring users to activate roles only when needed and with time-limited access, reducing the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must require multifactor authentication for all users
Description: Without the use of multifactor authentication (MFA), the ease of access to privileged and non-privileged functions is greatly increased. MFA provides an additional layer of security beyond passwords, significantly reducing the risk of unauthorized access through credential theft or compromise.
Notes: CAT I - High; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must block legacy authentication protocols
Description: Legacy authentication protocols such as POP, SMTP, IMAP, and MAPI do not support modern multifactor authentication. These protocols are frequently used as entry points by attackers. Blocking legacy authentication forces users to adopt modern authentication methods that support MFA.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must require phishing-resistant MFA for privileged role administrators
Description: Privileged accounts are high-value targets for attackers. Phishing-resistant MFA methods such as FIDO2 security keys or Windows Hello for Business provide the highest level of authentication assurance for administrative accounts, protecting against sophisticated phishing attacks.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must enable sign-in risk-based conditional access policies
Description: Sign-in risk policies use Azure AD Identity Protection to evaluate the risk level of each authentication attempt. Risky sign-ins from unfamiliar locations, anonymous IP addresses, or malware-linked IPs can be automatically blocked or require additional verification to prevent unauthorized access.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must enable user risk-based conditional access policies
Description: User risk policies use Azure AD Identity Protection to detect compromised user accounts. When a user account is determined to be at risk due to leaked credentials or suspicious behavior, the policy can require password changes or block access until the risk is remediated.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Entra ID must restrict guest user access permissions
Description: Guest users represent external identities granted access to the tenant. By default, guest users may have overly permissive access to directory information. Restricting guest user access ensures external users cannot enumerate users, groups, or other directory objects, reducing the risk of data exposure.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Entra ID V1R1

Title: Macros must be blocked from running in Access files from the Internet
Description: Macros in Access files obtained from the Internet could contain malicious code. Blocking macro execution from Internet-sourced files protects users from running untrusted code.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trust Bar Notifications for unsigned application add-ins in Access must be disabled and blocked
Description: Unsigned application add-ins may contain malicious code. Disabling Trust Bar notifications and blocking unsigned add-ins prevents users from inadvertently enabling unsafe code.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Access
Description: Without requiring digital signatures, malicious macros could be executed without user awareness. Blocking unsigned VBA macros ensures only trusted, signed code can run.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Macro Runtime Scan Scope must be enabled for all documents
Description: The Macro Runtime Scan Scope setting determines the behavior of the runtime scan for enabled macros across all Office applications. Enabling this scan helps detect malicious macro activity during execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Document metadata for rights managed Office Open XML files must be protected
Description: When an Office Open XML document is protected with Information Rights Management, the metadata of the document should also be encrypted. Failure to protect metadata could expose sensitive information about the document.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Office client must be prevented from polling the SharePoint Server for published links
Description: Polling the SharePoint server for published links can create unnecessary network traffic and potential information disclosure. This capability should be disabled when not required.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Custom user interface (UI) code must be blocked from loading in all Office applications
Description: Custom UI code, such as code behind forms and controls, can be used to execute malicious actions. Blocking custom UI code loading prevents potential exploitation of the Office application interface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: ActiveX Controls must be initialized in Safe Mode
Description: ActiveX controls can be used to run arbitrary code. Initializing ActiveX controls in Safe Mode limits the capabilities of controls, preventing them from accessing system resources or executing potentially dangerous operations.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Macros in all Office applications opened programmatically must follow macro security level
Description: When Office applications are opened programmatically, macros may bypass normal security settings. Enforcing macro security levels for programmatically opened files ensures consistent security across all access methods.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trust Bar notifications must display information about automatically blocked content
Description: Trust Bar notifications inform users when potentially dangerous content has been automatically blocked. Displaying this information ensures users are aware of security actions taken by the application.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Office applications must specify encryption type in password-protected Office 97-2003 files
Description: Password-protected Office 97-2003 files must use a specified encryption type to ensure adequate protection. Weak or default encryption algorithms may not provide sufficient security for sensitive documents.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Office applications must specify encryption type in password-protected Office Open XML files
Description: Specifying the encryption type for password-protected Office Open XML files ensures that strong encryption algorithms are used, providing adequate confidentiality protection for document contents.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Users must be prevented from creating new trusted locations in the Trust Center
Description: Trusted locations bypass security checks on files. Allowing users to add trusted locations increases the risk that malicious content could be opened without security scanning.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Office applications must not load XML expansion packs with Smart Documents
Description: Smart Document XML expansion packs can contain code that runs when the document is opened. Blocking these packs prevents potential code execution from untrusted sources.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The load of controls in Forms3 must be blocked
Description: Controls in Forms3 could contain malicious code. Blocking the loading of these controls prevents potential code execution and reduces the application attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Add-on Management must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs
Description: Add-on management controls the loading and execution of browser add-ons within Office programs. Enabling this feature ensures unauthorized add-ons cannot compromise the security of Office applications.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Consistent MIME handling must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs
Description: Consistent MIME handling ensures that files are identified and handled correctly based on their actual content type rather than their file extension, preventing potential exploitation through file type mismatches.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: User name and password must be disabled in all Office programs
Description: Disabling the use of user name and password in Office programs prevents credentials from being stored or transmitted insecurely through Office application features.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Information Bar must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: The Information Bar provides security notifications to users about potentially dangerous content or actions. Disabling it would prevent users from receiving important security warnings.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Local Machine Zone Lockdown restricts the permissions of content running in the Local Machine zone, preventing potentially malicious scripts from having elevated access to system resources.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The MIME Sniffing safety feature must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: MIME sniffing examines file content to determine its actual type regardless of the declared MIME type. Enabling this feature prevents files from being misidentified and processed incorrectly.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Navigate URL must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Navigate URL security feature controls how URLs are opened from Office applications. Proper configuration ensures URLs are handled securely and prevents unauthorized navigation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Object Caching Protection must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Object caching protection prevents ActiveX control data from being cached and reused across security contexts, reducing the risk of cross-domain data leakage.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Protection from zone elevation must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Zone elevation protection prevents content in a lower security zone from navigating to a higher security zone, blocking privilege escalation attacks through zone manipulation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: ActiveX installation restriction must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Restricting ActiveX installation prevents unauthorized ActiveX controls from being installed through Office programs, reducing the attack surface and preventing potential code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File Download Restriction must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Restricting file downloads from within Office programs prevents potentially malicious files from being downloaded and executed without proper security scanning.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Save from URL feature must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: The Save from URL security feature controls how files from URLs are saved, ensuring proper security checks are applied before files are saved to the local system.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Scripted Windows Security restrictions must be enabled in all Office programs
Description: Scripted Windows Security restrictions prevent scripts from manipulating browser windows in ways that could deceive users or bypass security controls.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Flash player activation must be disabled in all Office programs
Description: Adobe Flash Player has a history of security vulnerabilities. Disabling Flash player activation in Office programs eliminates this attack vector and reduces the application attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Excel
Description: Allowing trusted locations on a network can enable remote content to bypass security checks. Disabling network trusted locations ensures that only local trusted content is exempt from security verification.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Excel
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in Excel may contain malicious code. Requiring digital signatures ensures that macros come from a trusted source and have not been tampered with.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel must be blocked
Description: DDE server launch can be exploited to execute arbitrary commands on the system. Blocking DDE server launch prevents attackers from using Excel as a vector for command execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel must be blocked
Description: DDE server lookup enables communication between applications that can be exploited. Blocking DDE server lookup prevents potential data exfiltration and command execution through DDE channels.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of dBase III / IV format files must be blocked in Excel
Description: Legacy dBase III/IV file formats may contain vulnerabilities that can be exploited. Blocking these legacy formats prevents potential exploitation through maliciously crafted files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Dif and Sylk format files must be blocked in Excel
Description: DIF and SYLK file formats are legacy formats that may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these formats reduces the attack surface of Excel.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked
Description: Excel 2 format macrosheets and add-in files are obsolete formats that may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents exploitation of legacy file format parsers.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 2 worksheets must be blocked
Description: Excel 2 format worksheets are obsolete and may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these legacy formats reduces the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 3 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked
Description: Excel 3 format macrosheets and add-in files are obsolete formats with potential exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents exploitation through legacy format parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 3 worksheets must be blocked
Description: Excel 3 worksheets are legacy formats that may harbor exploitable vulnerabilities. These must be blocked to reduce the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 4 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked
Description: Excel 4.0 macrosheets (XLM macros) are a known attack vector used by adversaries. Blocking these formats prevents exploitation of the legacy Excel 4.0 macro system.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 4 workbooks must be blocked
Description: Excel 4 workbooks are obsolete formats that may be used to deliver malicious content. Blocking these formats prevents exploitation through legacy file parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 4 worksheets must be blocked
Description: Excel 4 worksheets are legacy formats that could contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents potential exploitation through maliciously crafted files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 95 workbooks must be blocked
Description: Excel 95 workbooks are obsolete formats with known vulnerabilities. Blocking these legacy formats prevents exploitation through file format parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Excel 95-97 workbooks and templates must be blocked
Description: Excel 95-97 format workbooks and templates are legacy formats that may harbor exploitable vulnerabilities in their file parsers. Blocking reduces the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The default file block behavior must be set to not open blocked files in Excel
Description: When file block settings prevent opening a file type, the default behavior should prevent the file from opening rather than opening it in Protected View, ensuring blocked formats cannot be accessed.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/save of Web pages and Excel 2003 XML spreadsheets must be blocked
Description: Web pages and Excel 2003 XML spreadsheets can contain scripts and active content that may execute malicious code. Blocking prevents potential exploitation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Extraction options must be blocked when opening corrupt Excel workbooks
Description: When opening corrupt workbooks, data extraction options could be exploited to bypass security controls. Blocking extraction options prevents potential exploitation of corrupt file handling.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Updating of links in Excel must be prompted and not automatic
Description: Automatic updating of links could allow external data sources to inject malicious content. Requiring user prompts before updating links ensures users are aware of external data access.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Loading of pictures from Web pages not created in Excel must be disabled
Description: Loading external pictures can be used for tracking or delivering malicious content. Disabling picture loading from non-Excel web pages prevents potential information disclosure and exploitation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: AutoRepublish in Excel must be disabled
Description: AutoRepublish automatically publishes workbook data to a web server. This could inadvertently expose sensitive data. Disabling AutoRepublish prevents unintended data exposure.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: AutoRepublish warning alert in Excel must be enabled
Description: If AutoRepublish is used, the warning alert must be enabled to notify users when workbook data is being published, ensuring awareness of data sharing activities.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File extensions must be enabled to match file types in Excel
Description: Enforcing file extension matching prevents files with mismatched extensions from being opened, which could be used to bypass security controls and deliver malicious content.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Scan of encrypted macros in Excel Open XML workbooks must be enabled
Description: Encrypted macros in Open XML workbooks could hide malicious code from security scanning. Enabling scanning of encrypted macros ensures comprehensive threat detection.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File validation in Excel must be enabled
Description: File validation checks the binary file format structure before opening, detecting potentially malicious files with corrupted or manipulated format structures.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: WEBSERVICE Function Notification in Excel must be configured to disable all with notifications
Description: The WEBSERVICE function can connect to external web services. Disabling it with notifications prevents unauthorized external connections while alerting users to attempted connections.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Macros must be blocked from running in Excel files from the Internet
Description: Excel files downloaded from the Internet may contain malicious macros. Blocking macro execution in Internet-sourced files prevents potential malware execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trust Bar notification must be enabled for unsigned application add-ins in Excel and blocked
Description: Unsigned add-ins may contain malicious code. Trust Bar notifications inform users when unsigned add-ins attempt to load, and blocking prevents them from executing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted Microsoft Query files must be blocked from opening in Excel
Description: Microsoft Query files can connect to external data sources. Blocking untrusted Query files prevents potential data exfiltration and unauthorized database access.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted database files must be opened in Excel in Protected View mode
Description: Database files can contain macros and code that execute when opened. Opening untrusted database files in Protected View prevents automatic code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files from Internet zone must be opened in Excel in Protected View mode
Description: Files downloaded from the Internet may contain malicious content. Opening them in Protected View provides a sandboxed environment that prevents code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files from unsafe locations must be opened in Excel in Protected View mode
Description: Files from locations designated as unsafe should be opened in Protected View to prevent malicious content from executing and potentially compromising the system.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files failing file validation must be opened in Excel in Protected View mode and disallow edits
Description: Files that fail validation checks may have been tampered with. Opening them in Protected View with editing disabled prevents potential exploitation of file format vulnerabilities.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File attachments from Outlook must be opened in Excel in Protected View mode
Description: Email attachments are a common malware delivery vector. Opening Outlook attachments in Protected View provides a sandboxed environment that prevents code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The SIP security mode in Lync must be enabled
Description: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) security mode ensures that all SIP communications are encrypted using TLS, protecting signaling traffic from interception and tampering.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The HTTP fallback for SIP connection in Lync must be disabled
Description: HTTP fallback for SIP connections allows unencrypted communication when TLS is unavailable. Disabling this fallback ensures communications always use encrypted channels.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Exchange client authentication with Exchange servers must use Kerberos Password Authentication
Description: Kerberos authentication provides stronger security than NTLM for Exchange client connections. Requiring Kerberos ensures encrypted, mutual authentication between client and server.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Outlook must use RPC encryption to communicate with Microsoft Exchange servers
Description: RPC encryption protects the confidentiality of data transmitted between Outlook and Exchange servers. Without encryption, sensitive email data could be intercepted in transit.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Scripts associated with public folders must be prevented from execution in Outlook
Description: Public folders can contain scripts placed by any authorized user. Preventing script execution from public folders mitigates the risk of running malicious scripts.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Scripts associated with shared folders must be prevented from execution in Outlook
Description: Shared folders may contain scripts from other users. Preventing script execution from shared folders protects against potential malicious code from trusted but potentially compromised sources.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files dragged from an Outlook e-mail to the file system must be created in ANSI format
Description: Creating dragged files in ANSI format rather than Unicode prevents potential exploitation of Unicode-related vulnerabilities when handling email attachments.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The junk email protection level must be set to No Automatic Filtering
Description: The junk email protection level controls how Outlook handles suspected junk mail. The setting ensures consistent handling without automatically moving messages.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: ActiveX One-Off forms must only be enabled to load with Outlook Controls
Description: ActiveX One-Off forms can contain malicious code. Restricting them to load only with Outlook Controls limits the attack surface and prevents exploitation through custom forms.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Outlook must be configured to prevent users overriding attachment security settings
Description: Users overriding attachment security settings could inadvertently allow malicious attachments to execute. Preventing overrides ensures consistent application of attachment security policies.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Internet must not be included in Safe Zone for picture download in Outlook
Description: Including the Internet in the Safe Zone for automatic picture downloads can be exploited for tracking and malicious content delivery. Internet sources must be excluded from safe zones.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Publish to Global Address List (GAL) button must be disabled in Outlook
Description: Publishing user certificates to the GAL could expose certificate information unnecessarily. Disabling this feature prevents unintended certificate publication.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The minimum encryption key length in Outlook must be at least 168 bits
Description: Encryption key lengths below 168 bits do not provide adequate security for sensitive data. Setting a minimum of 168 bits ensures that email encryption provides sufficient cryptographic strength.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The warning about invalid digital signatures must be enabled in Outlook
Description: Users must be warned when digital signatures on emails are invalid. Without this warning, users may trust emails with forged or expired certificates.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Outlook must retrieve CRLs always when online
Description: Certificate Revocation List (CRL) checking ensures that revoked certificates are detected. Always checking CRLs when online prevents use of compromised certificates.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Outlook Security Mode must use the Outlook Security Group Policy
Description: Using Outlook Security Group Policy ensures centralized management of security settings. Without this, individual users could weaken their security configuration.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The ability to demote attachments from Level 2 to Level 1 must be disabled
Description: Demoting attachment security levels could allow potentially dangerous file types to bypass security controls. This capability must be disabled to maintain consistent attachment handling.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The display of Level 1 attachments must be disabled in Outlook
Description: Level 1 attachments include potentially dangerous file types such as executables. Disabling their display prevents users from inadvertently opening malicious files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Level 1 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered
Description: Level 1 file attachments include executable and script files that pose a high security risk. Blocking delivery of these attachments prevents potential malware execution.
Notes: CAT I - High; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Level 2 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered
Description: Level 2 file attachments include file types that pose moderate risk. Blocking delivery ensures these potentially dangerous files cannot reach users.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Outlook must not run scripts in forms with script and layout in the message
Description: Forms containing both script and layout in the email message could execute malicious code when opened. Preventing script execution in such forms mitigates this risk.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Custom actions using the Outlook object model must be automatically denied
Description: The Outlook object model allows programmatic control of Outlook. Custom actions using this model must be denied to prevent malicious automation of email operations.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted programs accessing Address Book via Outlook object model must be denied
Description: The Address Book contains contact information that could be harvested by malware. Denying untrusted access to the Address Book prevents unauthorized data exfiltration.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Binding Address Information to custom fields in custom forms must be denied
Description: Binding address information to custom fields could allow data harvesting through specially crafted forms. Denying this capability prevents unauthorized collection of contact data.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted programs using Save As to save items programmatically must be denied
Description: The Save As command could be used by malicious programs to save email content to unauthorized locations. Denying this prevents data exfiltration through programmatic file saving.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted programs accessing recipient fields must be denied
Description: Recipient fields contain email addresses that could be harvested by malware. Denying untrusted access to these fields prevents unauthorized collection of email addresses.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted programs sending email via Response method must be denied
Description: The Response method on task or meeting requests could be exploited to send unauthorized emails. Denying untrusted programs from using this method prevents email spoofing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Untrusted programs sending email via Outlook object model must be denied
Description: The Outlook object model allows programmatic sending of emails. Denying untrusted programs from sending email through Outlook prevents malware from using Outlook as a spam relay.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Hyperlinks in suspected phishing messages must not be allowed
Description: Hyperlinks in phishing messages can direct users to malicious websites. Disabling hyperlinks in suspected phishing messages prevents users from inadvertently visiting harmful sites.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The Security Level for macros in Outlook must warn for signed and disable unsigned
Description: Macro security in Outlook should warn users about signed macros while blocking unsigned ones entirely. This ensures only verified macros can execute with user consent.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Project
Description: Network trusted locations in Project could allow remote content to bypass security checks. Disabling them ensures only local trusted content is exempt from verification.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Project must automatically disable unsigned add-ins without informing users
Description: Unsigned add-ins in Project may contain malicious code. Automatically disabling them without notification prevents users from enabling potentially dangerous add-ins.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Project
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in Project may contain malicious code. Blocking them ensures only trusted, signed code can execute within Project files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in PowerPoint
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in PowerPoint presentations may contain malicious code. Blocking them ensures only trusted, signed macros can execute.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The ability to run programs from PowerPoint must be disabled
Description: PowerPoint can be configured to run external programs through action buttons and links. Disabling this prevents PowerPoint from being used as a vector for launching malicious programs.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of PowerPoint 97-2003 presentations, shows, templates, and add-in files must be blocked
Description: Legacy PowerPoint formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these formats prevents exploitation through maliciously crafted legacy presentation files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The default file block behavior must be set to not open blocked files in PowerPoint
Description: When file block settings prevent opening a file type, the default behavior should prevent opening rather than using Protected View, ensuring blocked formats cannot be accessed.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Encrypted macros in PowerPoint Open XML presentations must be scanned
Description: Encrypted macros could hide malicious code from security scanning. Enabling scanning of encrypted macros ensures comprehensive threat detection in PowerPoint files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File validation in PowerPoint must be enabled
Description: File validation checks the binary file format structure before opening, detecting potentially malicious files with corrupted or manipulated format structures in PowerPoint.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Macros from the Internet must be blocked from running in PowerPoint
Description: PowerPoint files from the Internet may contain malicious macros. Blocking macro execution in Internet-sourced files prevents potential malware execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Unsigned add-ins in PowerPoint must be blocked with no Trust Bar Notification
Description: Unsigned add-ins may contain malicious code. Blocking them without Trust Bar notification prevents users from enabling potentially dangerous add-ins.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files downloaded from the Internet must be opened in Protected View in PowerPoint
Description: Internet-downloaded files may contain malicious content. Opening them in Protected View provides a sandboxed environment preventing code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: PowerPoint attachments opened from Outlook must be in Protected View
Description: Email attachments are a common malware delivery vector. Opening Outlook attachments in Protected View provides sandbox protection against malicious content.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files in unsafe locations must be opened in Protected View in PowerPoint
Description: Files from unsafe locations should be opened in Protected View to prevent malicious content from executing and compromising the system.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: If file validation fails, files must be opened in Protected View in PowerPoint with edit disabled
Description: Files that fail validation may have been tampered with. Opening them in Protected View with editing disabled prevents exploitation of file format vulnerabilities.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The use of network locations must be ignored in PowerPoint
Description: Network trusted locations in PowerPoint could allow remote content to bypass security checks. Ignoring network locations ensures only local content is trusted.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Publisher must prompt when another application programmatically opens a macro
Description: When another application opens a macro in Publisher programmatically, the user should be prompted to ensure they are aware of and consent to the macro execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Publisher must automatically disable unsigned add-ins without informing users
Description: Unsigned add-ins in Publisher may contain malicious code. Automatically disabling them prevents potential exploitation without requiring user interaction.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Publisher must disable all unsigned VBA macros
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in Publisher may contain malicious code. Disabling all unsigned macros ensures only verified, signed code can execute.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Visio
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in Visio may contain malicious code. Blocking ensures only trusted, signed macros can execute within Visio files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Visio
Description: Network trusted locations in Visio could allow remote content to bypass security checks. Disabling them ensures only local content is exempt from security verification.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Visio must automatically disable unsigned add-ins without informing users
Description: Unsigned add-ins in Visio may contain malicious code. Automatically disabling them prevents users from enabling potentially dangerous add-ins.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Visio 2000-2002 Binary Drawings, Templates and Stencils must be blocked
Description: Legacy Visio 2000-2002 binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these formats prevents exploitation through legacy file format parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Visio 2003-2010 Binary Drawings, Templates and Stencils must be blocked
Description: Legacy Visio 2003-2010 binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these formats reduces the application attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Visio 5.0 or earlier Binary Drawings, Templates and Stencils must be blocked
Description: Visio 5.0 and earlier binary formats are extremely old and may contain multiple exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents exploitation of obsolete format parsers.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Macros must be blocked from running in Visio files from the Internet
Description: Visio files from the Internet may contain malicious macros. Blocking macro execution in Internet-sourced files prevents potential malware execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Word must automatically disable unsigned add-ins without informing users
Description: Unsigned add-ins in Word may contain malicious code. Automatically disabling them prevents users from enabling potentially dangerous add-ins.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: In Word, encrypted macros must be scanned
Description: Encrypted macros in Word documents could hide malicious code from security scanning. Enabling scanning ensures comprehensive threat detection.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files downloaded from the Internet must be opened in Protected View in Word
Description: Internet-downloaded files may contain malicious content. Opening them in Protected View provides a sandboxed environment preventing code execution.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Files located in unsafe locations must be opened in Protected View in Word
Description: Files from unsafe locations should be opened in Protected View to prevent malicious content from executing and potentially compromising the system.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: If file validation fails, files must be opened in Protected View in Word with edit disabled
Description: Files that fail validation checks may have been tampered with. Opening them in Protected View with editing disabled prevents potential exploitation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Word attachments opened from Outlook must be in Protected View
Description: Email attachments are a common malware delivery vector. Opening Word Outlook attachments in Protected View provides sandbox protection.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: The default file block behavior must be set to not open blocked files in Word
Description: When file block settings prevent a file type from opening, the default behavior should prevent the file from opening entirely rather than opening in Protected View.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 2 and earlier binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 2 and earlier binary formats are obsolete and may contain exploitable vulnerabilities in their file parsers. Blocking prevents exploitation.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 2000 binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 2000 binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these legacy formats reduces the attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 2003 binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 2003 binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents exploitation through legacy file format parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 2007 and later binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Binary document formats can contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking Word 2007+ binary formats encourages use of safer Open XML formats.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 6.0 binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 6.0 binary formats are obsolete and may contain multiple exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking prevents exploitation of legacy format parsers.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 95 binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 95 binary formats are legacy formats that may harbor exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking reduces the application attack surface.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word 97 binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word 97 binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities in their file format parsers. Blocking prevents exploitation through maliciously crafted files.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Open/Save of Word XP binary documents and templates must be blocked
Description: Word XP binary formats may contain exploitable vulnerabilities. Blocking these legacy formats prevents exploitation through file format parsing.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: In Word, macros must be blocked from running even if Enable all macros is selected
Description: Even if the macro security setting allows all macros, Internet-sourced macros should still be blocked. This provides defense-in-depth against macro-based malware in Word.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Word
Description: Network trusted locations in Word could allow remote content to bypass security checks. Disabling ensures only local trusted content is exempt from security verification.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Word
Description: Unsigned VBA macros in Word may contain malicious code. Requiring digital signatures ensures macros come from a trusted source and have not been tampered with.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5

Title: File validation in Word must be enabled
Description: File validation checks the binary file format structure before opening, detecting potentially malicious Word files with corrupted or manipulated format structures.
Notes: CAT II - Medium; STIG: Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus V3R5
